title: Archived workspace contains no source, tests, or README — deliverables absent
evidence: find rep2/ -type f returns only TASK.md, stack.json, scores.json, _meta.json, _agent_stdout.log, _agent_stderr.log, .coverage, .hermes_usage.json — no app.py/test_app.py/README.md, though _agent_stdout.log claims all three were created. Sibling rep1 (same cell) archived all source. TASK.md deliverable 'Working source code in the workspace directory' is not present in the archive.
suggestion: Fix the runner's archive step so generated source is copied into run_dir; re-run or re-archive this cell. Conformance (R1–R11) cannot be verified until source is restored.

title: Stored scores cannot be corroborated: .coverage is empty and rep2 has no retort.db row
evidence: The 53KB .coverage SQLite DB has 0 rows in file/line_bits/arc (only meta version 7.15.1); retort.db has no experiment_runs row for python/s3/rep2 (only replicate 1, id=13). scores.json is therefore the sole score record: test_coverage=0.99, defect_rate=1.0, code_quality=0.789, idiomatic=0.4.
suggestion: Have the scorer flush coverage data into the archived .coverage (or drop the empty file), and ensure inline-gate runs are persisted to retort.db so scores are independently checkable.

title: Mechanical scores indicate the run itself built and passed tests — the defect is archival, not functional
evidence: scores.json test_coverage=0.99 (>0 ⇒ tests executed and ~all passed), defect_rate=1.0 (build+test succeeded); _agent_stdout.log reports 'Test results: 14/14 passed' with health, CRUD, ?author= filter, 400 validation, 404, 409 duplicate-ISBN cases. The generated code most likely satisfied the spec at scoring time but was not preserved in the archive.
suggestion: Treat this run as evaluation-blocked (source missing) rather than a functional failure when comparing across cells.
